description: In September 2021, El Salvador became the first country to adopt Bitcoin as legal tender, sparking a fierce debate about sovereignty, inflation, and financial inclusion. The initiative, led by President Nayib Bukele, served as a polarizing test case for developing nations integrating volatile crypto assets into national fiscal policy.

key_events.description: Bukele announces the Bitcoin Law at a conference in Miami.
Official rollout of 'Chivo' digital wallet begins.
Protests occur in San Salvador against the forced adoption of Bitcoin.
IMF formally warns El Salvador regarding the risks of Bitcoin adoption.

calibration_notes: Measures the impact of government-led 'top-down' crypto adoption versus grassroots skepticism.
